perturbation.original_text: For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter i) Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Thirty (30) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows: Source: VNUE, INC., 8-K, 9/14/2015 For every Five (5) music venues that sign a contract with VNUE, Six Hundred Thousand (600,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter; and If Ten (10) music venues sign a contract with VNUE before January 16, 2016, Promoter will receive an additional bonus of Three Hundred Thousand (300,000) shares of VNUE common stock.
perturbation.changed_text: For providing services as set forth herein, VNUE will compensate Promoter i) Two Thousand Five Hundred Dollars ($2,500.00) for each One (1) Year contract Promoter secures per venue and Five Thousand Dollars ($5,000,00) for each Two (2) Year contract Promoter secures per venue, with payment due to Promoter within Fifteen (15) Days from the date on which each such contract is countersigned; and ii) through the issuance of VNUE common stock as set forth below, and for the purposes of Rule 144 such shares of stock shall be deemed to have fully earned by Promoter upon the date of each issuance of such stock certificates by VStock Transfer: Three Million (3,000,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter for performing Promotion Services as follows: Source: VNUE, INC., 8-K, 9/14/2015 For every Five (5) music venues that sign a contract with VNUE, Six Hundred Thousand (600,000) shares of VNUE common stock shall be awarded to Promoter; and If Ten (10) music venues sign a contract with VNUE before January 16, 2016, Promoter will receive an additional bonus of Three Hundred Thousand (300,000) shares of VNUE common stock.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 2 modifies the payment deadline for the monetary compensation from 'Thirty (30) Days' to 'Fifteen (15) Days'. This creates an in-text contradiction, because later in Section 3, it states VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting. Because the compensation is different, it is unclear when the payment has to be made, fifteen days or thirty days.
perturbation.contradicted_text: VNUE shall reimburse Promoter for expenses incurred by Promoter while performing the duties herein. Promoter shall deliver to VNUE an itemized accounting of expenses incurred on a weekly basis, and VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline for VNUE's compensation to the promoter from 'Thirty (30) Days' to 'Fifteen (15) Days'. The contradicted text states: 'VNUE shall reimburse Promoter within Thirty (30) Days of receipt of such accounting.' This creates a direct contradiction within the contract because it is now unclear whether VNUE has 15 days or 30 days to reimburse the promoter, leading to potential disputes and unenforceability. The modification introduces an inconsistency, directly violating the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: At any time prior to the end of the Term, the Parties may agree in writing to extend the Agreement for successive One (1) Year periods (the "Renewal Terms") under the same conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: At any time prior to the end of the Term, VNUE may extend the Agreement for successive One (1) Year periods (the "Renewal Terms") under the same conditions set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 6 removes the need for both parties to agree in writing to extend the agreement, instead it makes it so that only VNUE needs to agree. This creates an in-text contradiction because later in Section 11, it states It is agreed between the parties that there are no other agreements or understandings between them relating to the subject matter of this agreement. Removing the need for both parties to agree would create an in-text contradiction due to one party would be deciding on the matter of the agreement's renewal period, instead of having both parties agreeing to it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is agreed between the parties that there are no other agreements or understandings between them relating to the subject matter of this agreement. This agreement supersedes all prior agreements, oral or written, between the parties and is intended as a complete and exclusive statement of the agreement between the parties. If any provision in this Agreement is held by a court of competent jurisdiction to be invalid, void or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue in full force without being impaired or invalidated in any way.
perturbation.justification: The original text required both parties to agree in writing to extend the agreement. The changed text allows only VNUE to extend the agreement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states, 'It is agreed between the parties that there are no other agreements or understandings between them relating to the subject matter of this agreement,' because VNUE unilaterally deciding to extend the agreement would create an understanding not agreed upon by both parties, thus contradicting the intention of the original agreement as described in the contradicted text. The change creates a clear inconsistency regarding the process of agreement renewal, leading to potential disputes and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Promoter agrees to indemnify VNUE for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions of Promoter taken in the performance of this Agreement. VNUE agrees to indemnify and hold Promoter harmless for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions, during the term of this Agreement, of VNUE officers, directors, employees, agents or third parties not under the control of Promoter.
perturbation.changed_text: Promoter agrees to indemnify VNUE for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions of Promoter taken in the performance of this Agreement. VNUE is not liable to indemnify and hold Promoter harmless for any and all liability or loss arising in any way out of the actions, during the term of this Agreement, of VNUE officers, directors, employees, agents or third parties not under the control of Promoter.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 7 modifies VNUE's indemnity obligations to the promoter, stating that VNUE is not liable to indemnify the promoter for any liability or loss, removing the original liability agreement. This creates an in-text contradiction, because later in Section 13, it states The party in violation of any of the provision agrees to pay to the injured party all court fees, attorney fees, charges and expenses as are deemed fair by the court. Due to the perturbation, there would be an in-text contradiction on who would be liable for actions during the term of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their respective successors, heirs and assigns. In case any provision of this Agreement shall be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, the validity, legality and enforceability of the remaining provisions of the Agreement shall not in any way be affected or impaired thereby. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Nevada without giving effect to choice of law doctrine. The party in violation of any of the provision agrees to pay to the injured party all court fees, attorney fees, charges and expenses as are deemed fair by the court. Each party hereto consents to personal jurisdiction in Nevada and voluntarily submits to its jurisdiction in any action or proceeding with respect to this Agreement. Venue for any action arising hereunder shall lie in the state and federal courts located Nevada.
perturbation.justification: The original text included VNUE's agreement to indemnify and hold Promoter harmless for losses arising from the actions of VNUE's officers, directors, etc. The changed text explicitly states that VNUE is 'not liable to indemnify' the promoter. This directly contradicts the later clause in Section 13, stating, 'The party in violation of any of the provision agrees to pay to the injured party all court fees, attorney fees, charges and expenses as are deemed fair by the court.' Because the indemnification obligation has been removed in the changed text, there is now a direct conflict regarding who is liable for losses and associated expenses, creating an in-text contradiction.
